It is also hot in Boston.
I believe it.

although...

We can get started.

I'm sweltering.

it?
So certain plants can survive under certain conditions, and so they categorize those hardiness zones slightly differently.
So there's an alphanumerical range, but then there's also a range that's based on general weather patterns.
So there's, like, humid continental, and then humid subtropical, and then tropical and subtropical.
So I think it has more to do with, like, I think the general, like, climatic trends over time.
And I think that kind of, like, the only real implications for that are, like, number one, how comfortable is it as a person?
But number two, like, what plants can actually survive there?
So now I think, like, in New York, it's probably some warmer weather plants would be able to survive.
Interesting.
Whereas in our climate...
That zone, I think, is like, I can't remember, but we were looking into this recently because we do a lot of gardening, but I could probably look it up and let you know if you're interested.
We could too, but that was actually so helpful.
I didn't expect you to have such a thorough summary.

Excellent.
All right.
So, yeah, just diving quickly into the performance side of things.
Generally, overall, all types of pages kind of held their usual performance range, no major changes in anything.

So it's just one of the consequences of the update that happened last month or so.
With the tuning pages, we saw a jump in sessions back into the usual average.
Most of it was driven by the cash flow statement blog performing quite well.
And then in terms of views, we saw cash flow statement and also LIFO receiving quite a bunch.
So that explains a little spike and then.
Second batch of tuning pages, quarterly taxes jumped up.

So that was the major, you know, there's no major change in sessions, it was just the views that sort of jumped up there.
Treasure pages, again, pretty much held in the range we've been seeing, no major changes observed.
For vendor pages, sessions held pretty much the same as last week.
Some of the newer pages got some hits.
We had the DevOps page receive about four sessions, so that's pretty nice.
Generally, it seems like things are taking shape over here as we publish and generate more of these pages.
Moving on to the talking points.
So we're working on the two editorial reflections this week.
Just want to clarify, Matt, the reason we're working two at a time is just we're kind of balancing the using these pages along with the vendor pages.
So that's that.
And then thank you.
Thank
We're sending over the Sample 5 articles, so we just want to be a little conscious of the tone.
Obviously, we've been swinging a bit back and forth.
We just want to land in center now, so that should help.
The vendor page updates, we've begun working on publishing the 34 pages.
We expect to get done early Friday.
Thanks, Victoria, for sending over the data wrapper IDs.

I think the overview pages are done for the content creation and a few of them for web hosting, I think.
So he's reviewing them.
We'll keep you updated as and when they get published.
We addressed all edits.
So I created it this morning before doing it.
That's why it says will address.

Noted your Shopify comments as well.
And I think the Shopify page has been drafted already, so we didn't include the line chart.
It just has a pie chart.
chart.

Everywhere the tone was pretty neutral, so pretty good on that.

So that's going pretty well.
I'm just going to pause there.
Any questions or anything you can answer about English, please?
No questions, that's all clear and sounds good.
I also wanted to acknowledge and thank you guys last week for publishing all those pages.
know, first of all, there were lots of tables, so lots of work there, and I sent more pages than I did initially, so thanks.
Thank you.
Thank You know what, it was good.
Thank you.

It was just a fun exercise for us.
That's very kind.

So it was a good exercise.

Yeah, so I believe that you can import JSON directly into Sanity through a CLI, but it does kind of defeat the purpose of using a component-based CMS in the first place, because all of the styling is tightly scoped to the individual components.
So I think, you know, this has been on our web engineering team's board for like two months, because it's not quite as simple as it seems to...
A table component for Sanity or for Contentful or like any of those just kind of sucks.

Yeah.
Okay.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly check-in between Ramp and GrowthX to discuss content performance, ongoing projects, and address any questions or concerns.](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content performance remains stable across most page types, with slight fluctuations in specific areas](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=268.0)
  - [Progress on vendor pages and editorial reflections is on track, with 34 new pages to be published by Friday](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
  - [Table formatting in Sanity CMS remains a challenge; web engineering team is working on a solution](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=585.0)
  - [Automation of vendor page publishing process to resume next week after internal projects wrap up](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Overview

  - [Blog pages: Sessions held steady, consistent with recent trends](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
  - [Per-diem pages: Slight drop, potentially due to Travelperk's UX improvements](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=295.0)
  - [Expense category: Continuing steady decline, limited options for improvement](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=317.0)
  - [Tuning pages: Session jump, driven by cash flow statement blog performance](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=340.0)
  - [Treasure pages: Maintained usual range](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=377.0)
  - [Vendor pages: Sessions stable, new pages (e.g., DevOps) gaining traction](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)

### Ongoing Projects and Updates

  - [Two editorial reflections in progress, balancing with vendor page creation](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
  - [Sample 5 articles being sent, aiming for a more centered tone](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [34 vendor pages to be published by early Friday](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
  - [Data wrapper IDs received and implementation begun](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)
  - [Overview pages for content creation and web hosting under review](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
  - [Shopify page drafted without line chart, as requested](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
  - [CRM and AI pages on track for delivery on B27](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)

### Table Formatting in Sanity CMS

  - [Current method for rendering tables is complex and not easily replicable](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
  - [Web engineering team exploring solutions for 2 months](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=644.0)
  - [Potential future solution: HTML or markdown copy-paste for entire tables](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=680.0)
  - [Matt to follow up with Luke on progress and potential editor changes](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=696.0)

### Vendor Page Publishing Automation

  - [Project temporarily slowed due to internal audit and other projects](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Full-speed work to resume early next week](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=730.0)
  - [Team anticipates progress updates soon](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Publish 34 vendor pages by early Friday](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
  - [Continue work on editorial reflections and Sample 5 articles](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
  - [Matt to follow up with Luke on table formatting solutions in Sanity CMS](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=696.0)
  - [Resume full-speed work on vendor page publishing automation next week](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=730.0)
  - [Deliver CRM and AI pages on B27](https://fathom.video/share/z6uKXzbo2KSdbo1bDMgNu4cpCV2tZn6X?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)

action_items.description: Follow up w/ Luke re table component issue in Sanity CMS (HTML/markdown copy-paste solution)
